name: improve-listing-selector
description: Redesign the ListingTypeSelector component to be visually clear and engaging

agents.description:
Redesign the ListingTypeSelector component at /Users/jimmycarrera/Documents/sources/nogal/nomades-webapp/src/app/components/ListingTypeSelector.tsx

Current problems:
- Both cards look identical (plain white, same border, same gray icons)
- No visual hierarchy — nothing draws the eye to either option
- Step icons are all gray and indistinct
- Cards feel flat, no sense of affordance or action
- No subtitle or supporting copy to help the user decide

Requirements:
- Keep all existing routing (href /listing/form/person and /listing/form/room) unchanged
- Keep all existing imports and framework (Tailwind CSS, FontAwesome, Next.js Link)
- Do NOT add new npm dependencies
- The Storybook story at ListingTypeSelector.stories.tsx should still work (no props needed)

Design direction:
1. Give each card a strong visual identity:
   - Person card (I Look for a Room): warm green/emerald accent (bg-success already defined). Add a colored top band or gradient header area.
   - Room card (I Have a Room to Rent): blue/info accent (bg-info already defined). Same treatment.
2. Make the cards feel like real CTAs — larger prominent title, visible arrow or chevron that animates on hover
3. Add a short subtitle under each heading (1 sentence) explaining the path
4. Make the step icons colored (matching the card accent) instead of all gray
5. Add hover lift effect: shadow + slight translateY on the cards
6. Mobile: stack vertically with good spacing
7. Add a short page heading above the grid: 'What would you like to do?'

Only edit ListingTypeSelector.tsx. Do not touch any other files.
